Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-15-2016 Text Ruling

Build Group, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Meredith Jones-McKeown of Sheppard Mullin Richter Hampton LLP (415-774-3278) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Michelle Ackerman of Steyer Lowenthal Law Offices (415-421-3400) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT BUILD GROUP, INC.'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is GRANTED on the conditions that: a) within five days it serves code-compliant verified responses to the deemed-served discovery stating all facts which support each of it cross-claims, identifying all persons with knowledge of those facts and the facts that they know, and producing all documents relating to such facts; b) within ten days making available for initial or further depositions all persons so identified to be deposed solely on the cross-claims; and c) Build Group not seek a trial continuance due to the granting of the motion. The proposed cross-complaint is based on the same basic facts as the complaint and answer filed by the parties and granting leave is supported by California's strong public policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings. Plaintiff has not shown that Build Group has acted in a dilatory manner nor, with the conditions imposed by the court, that it will be prejudiced by the granting of the motion. If plaintiff believes it needs further information or has any other needs that have arisen due to the grating of the motion, the parties should meet and confer on all such matters and may raise them by ex parte application. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, bus. tel: (530) 339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)
